title: Decoupled Knowledge Distillation
task: Reproduce the DKD experiment on CIFAR-100 with teacher=ResNet32x4 and student=ResNet8x4, reporting Top-1 accuracy.

model_components.name: Teacher: ResNet32x4
model_components.description: ResNet with width multiplier 4, used as the teacher network. Pre-trained on CIFAR-100 achieving 79.42% top-1 accuracy.

model_components.name: Student: ResNet8x4
model_components.description: ResNet with width multiplier 4 and fewer layers, used as the student network. Baseline (vanilla) top-1 accuracy is 72.50%.

algorithms.name: TCKD (Target Class Knowledge Distillation)
algorithms.description: Binary logit distillation for the target class. Defined as KL(b^T || b^S) where b = [p_t, p_{\t}] are binary probabilities of target vs. all non-target classes. p_t = exp(z_t) / sum_j exp(z_j), p_{\t} = 1 - p_t.

algorithms.name: NCKD (Non-Target Class Knowledge Distillation)
algorithms.description: Multi-category logit distillation among non-target classes. Defined as KL(p_hat^T || p_hat^S) where p_hat_i = exp(z_i) / sum_{j != t} exp(z_j) for i != t.

algorithms.name: DKD (Decoupled Knowledge Distillation)
algorithms.description: DKD = alpha * TCKD + beta * NCKD, scaled by T^2. Decouples the coupling in classical KD where NCKD weight was (1 - p_t^T). Uses independent hyperparameters alpha and beta for TCKD and NCKD respectively.

algorithms.name: Cross-Entropy Loss
algorithms.description: Standard cross-entropy loss between student predictions and ground-truth labels.

algorithms.name: Total Loss
algorithms.description: Total training loss = 1.0 * CE_loss + DKD_loss (alpha * TCKD + beta * NCKD) * T^2

training_config.lr_schedule: Step decay: divide by 10 at epochs 150, 180, 210
loss_functions: DKD loss = alpha*TCKD + beta*NCKD, scaled by T^2

extra.note_beta: beta=8.0 for ResNet32x4 teacher based on z_t - z_max gap of 8.40

preprocessing: Normalize with CIFAR-100 mean and std
implementation_notes: Teacher ResNet32x4 achieves 79.42% top-1 on CIFAR-100; student ResNet8x4 baseline is 72.50%.
Temperature T=4 for all CIFAR-100 experiments.
beta=8.0 for ResNet32x4 teacher (based on z_t - z_max gap of 8.40 as guidance).
A 20-epoch linear warmup is applied at the start of training because beta can be high leading to large initial loss.
The DKD loss is scaled by T^2 (as in classical KD practice).
Implementation follows the practice in CRD [33] (Tian et al., ICLR 2020).
For NCKD: softmax is computed over non-target logits only (excluding target class logit), using temperature T.
For TCKD: binary probabilities b=[p_t, p_{\t}] are computed with temperature T applied to logits.
The pseudo-code shows: pnct_stu = F.softmax(l_stu[1-t]/T), pnct_tea = F.softmax(l_tea[1-t]/T); dkd_loss = (alpha*tckd + beta*nckd) * T**2.
KD baseline for this pair achieves 73.33% top-1 (from Table 6).
DKD improvement over KD is +2.99% for this teacher-student pair.
